# W22 Probe S — asset network centrality on FTSE returns

*Auto-generated by `experiments/analyze_probe_s.py`.*

## Question

Are FTSE assets organized in a hub structure that hub-tilted portfolios could exploit?
Measure: eigenvector centrality + Gini concentration.

## Methodology

- Load FTSE paper-window returns (~1566 days × 98 assets)
- Slice into windows (full + first/last halves)
- Build correlation network (dense + top-K sparsified)
- Compute eigenvector centrality (power iteration)
- Report Gini coefficient (concentration measure)

## Window: full

Shape: (1566, 98)

| top_k_edges | Gini | max cent | min cent | top-5 asset indices |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 5 | 0.561 | 0.0604 | 0.0004 | [41, 0, 7, 96, 90] |
| 20 | 0.289 | 0.0272 | 0.0006 | [94, 59, 0, 74, 41] |
| 97 | 0.124 | 0.0135 | 0.0006 | [94, 0, 41, 59, 7] |

## Window: first_half

Shape: (783, 98)

| top_k_edges | Gini | max cent | min cent | top-5 asset indices |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 5 | 0.588 | 0.0634 | 0.0000 | [0, 41, 7, 96, 90] |
| 20 | 0.288 | 0.0254 | 0.0000 | [59, 94, 68, 0, 36] |
| 97 | 0.144 | 0.0139 | 0.0000 | [59, 0, 68, 94, 41] |

## Window: last_half

Shape: (783, 98)

| top_k_edges | Gini | max cent | min cent | top-5 asset indices |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 5 | 0.434 | 0.0439 | 0.0002 | [41, 7, 0, 37, 66] |
| 20 | 0.309 | 0.0242 | 0.0003 | [94, 54, 37, 41, 0] |
| 97 | 0.131 | 0.0135 | 0.0004 | [0, 41, 94, 78, 54] |

## Interpretation

- Mean Gini across windows (dense network): 0.133

🔴 **Homogeneous network** (Gini < 0.2): FTSE assets have similar centrality.
Hub-tilt strategy has limited theoretical headroom on this dataset.

## Caveat

Probe S characterizes the *structure* of the asset graph; it does NOT predict whether
hub-tilt would improve realized OOS hypervolume. That would require a separate run with
hub-tilted portfolio weights passed through the same walk-forward harness.
